summary: Re-tested HR module P0 flows after main agent's fixes (stripEmpty + apiErrorMessage in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js). The critical React crash from iter34 is FIXED. Onboarding 'only Name' submission no longer crashes — the empty-string optional fields are stripped client-side. Full completion flow auto-creates the employee (E-1009 verified). Employee360 renders all 7 tabs with no console errors, skill add works, certification with expiry 2026-06-15 shows 'expiring soon' badge with days countdown. Leave Apply → Approve flow works, calendar prev/next navigation correctly cycles months (May → April → June). No React 'Objects are not valid as a React child' errors observed anywhere.

integration_issues.issue: Balances tab rendered 0 rows for super_admin after applying+approving a 2-day leave for an employee. The tab likely shows balances scoped to the logged-in user's own employee record (admin@erp.com has no linked employee), not the employee whose leave was just approved. Backend balance reduction itself was already verified 100% in iter34 pytest. Consider adding an employee filter/dropdown to the Balances tab, or default to the most recently impacted employee for HR/super_admin roles.

issues: FROM/TO date inputs still use native <input type='date'> instead of shadcn Calendar/DatePicker (carry-over from iter34, low priority).

action_items: (Optional) Scope Leave Balances tab so super_admin/HR can pick the employee whose balances to inspect — currently appears empty for admin users with no employee record.
(Optional) Replace native date inputs in Leave Apply with shadcn Calendar component for consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js helpers (formatApiErrorDetail, apiErrorMessage, stripEmpty) are correctly used across all 3 HR pages — good unification.
hr_router.py leave_calendar now correctly includes the 3rd $or clause for leaves spanning the queried month.
hr_router.py is 874 lines — still above the 700-line guideline; recommend splitting into hr/onboarding_router.py, hr/employee360_router.py, hr/leave_router.py.

Onboarding 'Complete & Auto-Trigger' green panel (.bg-emerald-50) shares the same CSS class as completed-stage rows — testing/automation must scope to the 'Auto-Triggers Fired' header to disambiguate; not a bug but could be brittle for tests.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the previously-crashing flows; 95% overall (Balances tab empty for admin-user is a minor UX issue, not a regression)

seed_data_creation: Created onboarding TEST_OnlyName_<ts> (in_progress, only Name field) and TEST_FullFlow_97074 (completed → became employee E-1009 with PPE + induction). Added TEST_Skill_<ts> and TEST_Cert_<ts> (expiry 2026-06-15) to first employee in E360. Submitted+approved one 2026-02-10 to 2026-02-11 leave application.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter34 critical bugs are FIXED. The api.js stripEmpty()+apiErrorMessage() utilities are now wired across Onboarding/Employee360/Leave. Backend leave_calendar 3-clause $or is in place. If new HR features arrive, the only minor improvement left is making the Leave Balances tab employee-selectable for HR/admin viewers.
rca_of_the_issue: Iter34 crash root cause confirmed mitigated: Pydantic 422 detail arrays are now flattened to string before reaching toast.error via apiErrorMessage(err, fallback). Additionally, empty optional fields (email='') are stripped from POST payloads via stripEmpty(), so EmailStr/etc. no longer reject them in the first place. Both layers of defence verified end-to-end through the UI.
